title: PipeShare support queue actions wired (dbg136)
description:
dbg135 fixed context menu picks in the Customer support queue, but Request Connection and Start chat still appeared to do nothing after a click.

Root cause: menu onSelect did fire and the backend APIs were called, but the admin UI never reacted — no support widget for chat, no remote panel for pending invites, no chat-response SSE handler for admins, and API errors were swallowed silently.

This release wires admin Start chat and Request Connection through the customer-support module: opens the support widget or remote panel with a waiting status, loads chat when the customer accepts, and shows an alert if the API fails.

Who is affected: Horizon admins using PipeShare (and PipeSync via shared module) Customer support queue on OVH private server. SaaS when Mike applies the platform version in releases.html.
changeLog: Wire admin Start chat: open support widget, wait for customer accept via SSE chat-response.
Wire admin Request Connection: show remote panel with pending invite status before WebRTC starts.
Create admin remote panel on first invite instead of no-op when panel did not exist yet.
Surface support API failures with alert instead of silent void promises.
Portal runtime cache bust dbg136; PipeSync addons.manifest dbg136.
